title: Conditional Copy, Replace, Rename, Delete, and Batch · Mastery Check
summary: A production board compares near-miss TypeScript and AWS decisions for conditional copy, replace, rename, delete, and batch and accepts only runtime validation, bounded ownership, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit controlled AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for conditional TypeScript mutation protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, copy or replace with explicit source version, conditional evidence, metadata directive, encryption, checksum, tags, and destination validation.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, implement rename as version-aware copy, validation, metadata update, reference switch, and safe source deletion with recoverable intermediate states.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, use validators, version IDs, idempotency records, or application locks to detect stale concurrent mutation and expose 409 or 412 meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Copy Replace Metadata
steps.instruction:

content.text: A quiz TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 6510 during a private media entitlement change. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to model a version-aware non-atomic copy, validate, and delete rename workflow; retain keyboard and screen-reader progress, cancellation, error, and recovery evidence; then replace one typed fixture with unknown runtime input and prove rejection before AWS use. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, copy or replace with explicit source version, conditional evidence, metadata directive, encryption, checksum, tags, and destination validation.
